name: Webhook Catch Connector
description: Generic webhook endpoint for receiving HTTP POST events. Choose auth: None, HMAC (provide secret key), or API Key (provide API key).
use_cases: Receive events from any webhook source

entity.name: Webhook Catch
entity.description: Generic webhook connector for receiving HTTP POST events with configurable authentication

metadata.auth_help: Choose None (no validation), HMAC (provide secret—sender signs body with HMAC-SHA256, include in X-Webhook-Signature), or API Key (provide key—sender sends in X-API-Key header).

placeholders.{{ENDPOINT_SLUG}}: URL slug for the webhook endpoint
placeholders.{{AUTH_TYPE}}: Authentication type: none, hmac, or api_key
placeholders.{{SECRET_KEY}}: Secret for HMAC (encrypted). User provides; sender signs request body with HMAC-SHA256.

connector_package.description: A connector is a connection to a third party; actions describe what you can do with it. Webhook Catch is inbound ingestion via HTTP POST.
follow_up_notes: Runtime: platform WebhookCatchConnector (inbound HTTP receiver, HMAC/API key verification) — not a catalog outbound HTTP package.
Entity + custom_settings_schema + metadata.supported_auth_types are the declarative contract; native code only executes that contract.
connector_package.actions document platform_native ingress surface; HTTP route lives in workflows/API.
